macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 Slides 1% as Chip Selloff and Middle East Tensions Deepen
items.summary: U.S. stock index futures slid on Friday, with the S&P 500 falling 1.01% amid a deepening selloff in chip stocks and rising U.S.-Iran tensions, forcing investors to reassess the AI-driven rally [1][2].

items.title: Fed Policymakers Swell for Rate Hike as Inflation Debate Intensifies
items.summary: Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack joined a growing chorus of policymakers arguing rates may need to rise to combat persistent inflation, setting up a charged debate at the upcoming July meeting under Chairman Kevin Warsh [1].

items.title: Oil Hits One-Month High Near $85 as U.S.-Iran Conflict Threatens Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Brent crude nears $85 a barrel and WTI rose 3.50% as renewed hostilities between Washington and Tehran cloud the outlook for the Strait of Hormuz, prompting IEA chief Fatih Birol to warn of critical energy security risks [2][3].

items.title: Netflix Plunges and Alphabet Drops 2% Following Weak Forecast and AI Concerns
items.summary: Netflix shares fell sharply after adding a weak forecast that pressured markets, while Alphabet Inc. dropped 2.17% as a rotation out of recent rally winners gathered momentum and chip stocks faced their steepest weekly decline in over a year [1][2].

items.title: Apple Overtakes Nvidia to Regain World's Most Valuable Company Spot at $4.88T
items.summary: Apple regained the top spot as the world's most valuable company with a $4.88 trillion valuation, overtaking Nvidia after the chipmaker saw a 3.5% market value drop amid the broader tech selloff [3].

items.title: SpaceX Shares Drop 6.9% After Starship Launch Abort; Public Debut Expected Friday
items.summary: SpaceX shares fell up to 6.9% following an aborted Starship launch due to an engine problem, though investors remain focused on the company's public debut scheduled for Friday [5][7].

items.title: U.S. Import Prices Rise Unexpectedly in June; Inflation Cools Significantly in Monthly Report
items.summary: U.S. import prices unexpectedly rose in June, while the latest monthly inflation report showed price hikes slowed sharply in June due to cheaper energy, marking the first significant cooling in six years [1][4].

items.title: China Keeps Benchmark Lending Rates Unchanged for 14th Month Despite Soft Q2 Data
items.summary: China is set to leave benchmark lending rates unchanged for a 14th consecutive month in July, despite softer-than-expected second-quarter economic data highlighting uneven growth in the world's second-largest economy [1].

items.title: US Imposes 25% Tariffs on Some Brazilian Imports Over Unfair Trade Practices
items.summary: The U.S. plans to impose a 25% tariff on some Brazilian imports, including excluding beef, coffee, and rare-earth materials, after a yearlong investigation concluded Brazil engaged in unfair trade practices [3][4].

items.title: Regional Banks Deliver Broad Q2 Gains Alleviating Middle East War Concerns
items.summary: U.S. regional banks including U.S. Bancorp and PNC Financial delivered broad second-quarter gains relying on a lending rebound and strong fee income, alleviating concerns that the Middle East war would weigh on loan demand [1].

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Slips Below $63,000 Amid Global Risk-Off Selloff and Middle East Tensions
items.summary: Bitcoin dropped 5.7% to trade near $62,800 as investors fled risky assets following a sharp decline in tech stocks and renewed Middle East tensions, with US stocks also extending losses.

items.title: Polkadot Plunges 11% Breaking Below $2.05 Support Level
items.summary: Polkadot (DOT) collapsed to $2.02, falling 11% and breaking key support amid a broader crypto market selloff, exposing the psychological $2.00 level.

items.title: European Authorities Seize $1.51B Bitcoin-Mixing Service Cryptomixer
items.summary: Europol dismantled the crypto-mixing platform Cryptomixer, used by ransomware groups to launder bitcoin, seizing servers, data, and $29 million in BTC.

items.title: FTX to Distribute Roughly $900 Million to Creditors in Fifth Wave
items.summary: FTX will begin its fifth creditor payout of approximately $900 million on July 31, 2026, with cumulative payouts reaching 105% for Class 5 and 103% for Classes 6A/6B.

items.title: SBI Holdings Completes Majority Acquisition of Singapore Crypto Platform Coinhako
items.summary: SBI Holdings finalized its majority stake acquisition of Coinhako following MAS regulatory approval, deepening its footprint in Asia's crypto market.

items.title: Hyperliquid Token HYPE Falls 12% Amid Massive Profit-Taking by a16z-Linked Whales
items.summary: HYPE dropped 12% over 24 hours as whales suspected to be linked to a16z sold 421,800 tokens worth $25.3 million, triggering a sharp price decline.

items.title: Kaspersky Exposes OkoBot's 20-Module Crypto Wallet Attack Targeting Global Users
items.summary: Cybersecurity firm Kaspersky identified the OkoBot malware framework stealing wallet recovery phrases via social engineering and trojanized GitHub apps, affecting users in multiple countries.

items.title: Cardano Hands Core Protocol Development to Independent Teams Under CIP-1694
items.summary: Cardano shifted core protocol work from IOG to independent teams under Intersect, implementing CIP-1694 guardrails to decentralize development.

items.title: French Gambling Regulator Orders ISPs to Geoblock Polymarket
items.summary: France's gambling authority mandated ISPs to block Polymarket, citing illegal gambling and market manipulation concerns.

items.title: Bitcoin Spot ETFs See $132M Net Inflow for Fourth Consecutive Day
items.summary: Bitcoin spot ETFs recorded $132 million in net inflows yesterday, marking four straight days of positive flows, while Ethereum ETFs saw $36.7 million with BlackRock's ETHA leading.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Chinese Startup Moonshot AI Releases Kimi K3, World's Largest Open-Weight AI Model
items.summary: Moonshot AI unveiled Kimi K3, a 2.8-trillion-parameter open-weight model with a 1-million-token context window, claiming frontier-adjacent performance rivaling Anthropic's Fable and approaching OpenAI's capabilities.

items.title: xAI Confirms Grok 4.6 as 2-Trillion-Parameter Model with Training Finishing Next Week
items.summary: Elon Musk confirmed on X that xAI's next flagship, Grok 4.6, is a 2-trillion-parameter model (up from 1.5T in Grok 4.5), with initial training scheduled to complete next week.

items.title: OpenAI Unveils GPT-Red, Automated Red-Teaming LLM for GPT-5.6 Adversarial Training
items.summary: OpenAI released GPT-Red, an internal automated red-teaming model that reduced prompt injection success rates from 90%+ on GPT-5 to under 23% on GPT-5.6, achieving 84% attack success versus 13% for human red-teamers.

items.title: Nvidia Announces Cosmos 3 Edge Model and Expands Physical AI Ecosystem in Japan
items.summary: Nvidia launched Cosmos 3 Edge, a new AI model, while expanding its physical AI ecosystems in Japan to support robotics and industrial automation applications.

items.title: Thinking Machines Releases Inkling, 975B-Parameter Open-Weights Mixture-of-Experts Model
items.summary: Thinking Machines launched Inkling, an open-weights Mixture-of-Experts model with 975B total parameters (41B active), pretrained on 45 trillion tokens of text, images, audio, and video.

items.title: PrismML Launches Bonsai 27B, Consumer-Device Multimodal Model for Smartphones
items.summary: PrismML released Bonsai 27B, a 27-billion-parameter multimodal model available in bit binary and 1.bit ternary variants, optimized to run locally on consumer devices including smartphones.

items.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Sonnet 5 and Restores Fable/Mythos After Export Control Review
items.summary: Anthropic released Claude Sonnet 5 and restored access to its frontier Fable and Mythos models following a federal export control review that had temporarily restricted them.

items.title: Google DeepMind Makes Veo 3 AI Video Tool Widely Available on Vertex AI
items.summary: Google DeepMind expanded access to Veo 3, its most powerful AI video creation tool, making it available for all users on the Vertex AI platform.

items.title: SpaceX in Talks to Provide U.S. Defense with Billion-Dollar Data Center Capacity for AI
items.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX is negotiating to provide the U.S. Department of Defense with data center capacity worth billions of dollars to run AI models, according to Wall Street Journal reports.

items.title: Apple Overtakes Nvidia as World's Most Valuable Company Amid AI Outlook Reassessment
items.summary: Apple surpassed Nvidia on Friday to become the world's most valuable company, reshuffling tech rankings as investors reassess artificial intelligence investment outlooks.

items.title: Coatue Management Leads $3B Investment in Databricks Valuing Company at $188B
items.summary: Investment firm Coatue Management is leading a $3 billion investment in data analytics provider Databricks, valuing the company at $188 billion according to Wall Street Journal reports.

items.title: IBM Research Unveils Groundbreaking Analog AI Chip for Deep Neural Networks
items.summary: IBM Research announced a new analog AI chip demonstrating remarkable efficiency and accuracy in performing complex computations for deep neural networks.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Bethesda Confirms New Fallout Game Developed by Obsidian Entertainment
items.summary: Bethesda officially confirmed that Obsidian Entertainment, the studio behind Fallout: New Vegas, is returning to the post-apocalyptic universe to develop a new Fallout game, while Todd Howard's team plays The Elder Scrolls VI daily.

items.title: Battlefield 6 Season 4 Launches July 21 with Top Gun Collaboration
items.summary: EA and Battlefield Studios announced that Battlefield 6 Season 4, titled 'Pacific Front,' will arrive on July 21 on PC, PS5, and Xbox, featuring a collaboration with 'Top Gun: Maverick' starting August 18.

items.title: Zenless Zone Zero Version 3.1 Update Announced for Second Anniversary
items.summary: MiHoYo revealed that Zenless Zone Zero will receive its Version 3.1 update on July 29, 2026, celebrating the game's second anniversary with new agents, a permanent mode, and cross-platform rewards.

items.title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced Tops 3 Million Sales in One Week
items.summary: Ubisoft reported that Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced has sold over 3 million copies worldwide in just one week, surpassing the launch performance of Assassin's Creed Shadows.

items.title: Final Fantasy XIV Mobile Global Release Canceled, China Servers Closing
items.summary: LightSpeed Studios confirmed the global release of Final Fantasy XIV Mobile has been canceled, with servers in China scheduled to shut down on September 30, 2026, after less than a year of service.

items.title: Gallipoli WWI Shooter Gets August 20 Release Date
items.summary: Blackmill Games announced that its tactical World War I multiplayer shooter Gallipoli will launch on August 20 on PS5, Xbox Series X/S, and PC, featuring 25-vs-25 battles and full crossplay.

items.title: Nintendo Ends Mobile Support for Mario Kart Tour in September
items.summary: Nintendo is retreating further from mobile gaming by ending support for Mario Kart Tour in September, continuing its strategy to focus on console-exclusive experiences.

items.title: Blizzard Reverts Diablo 4 Season Changes After Player Backlash
items.summary: Blizzard released a patch for Diablo 4 that reverts almost all controversial changes from its disastrous new season, admitting that 'not every idea lands exactly as intended.'

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Overtakes Nvidia to Become World's Most Valuable Company
items.summary: Apple reclaimed the top spot as the world's most valuable company on Friday, surpassing Nvidia with a market capitalization approaching $5 trillion as investors reassess AI outlooks.

items.title: China Launches First Space-Computing Satellite Constellation
items.summary: Shanghai Xingshu Tiansuan Space Technology Co. launched the first constellation of a space-computing project on Saturday, aiming to deploy 1,000 satellites total.

items.title: SpaceX in Talks to Provide Billions in Data Center Capacity to U.S. Defense
items.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX is negotiating with the U.S. Department of Defense to provide data center capacity worth billions of dollars to run advanced models.

items.title: EU Forces Google to Open Android to Rival AI Chatbots and Share Search Data
items.summary: The European Union issued new rules requiring Google to open its Android system to rival AI companies and reveal internal search data to competitors.

items.title: Meta Approved to Lay Off Workers in AI Discrimination Case
items.summary: A U.S. judge rejected Meta employees' bid to block layoffs, allowing the tech giant to cut jobs while workers pursue claims that AI tools targeted them due to disabilities or medical leave.

items.title: Samsung's Upcoming Foldables Revealed in Leaked Marketing Images
items.summary: Leaked marketing images ahead of the July 22 Galaxy Unpacked event have revealed details of Samsung's upcoming foldable devices.

items.title: BrainCo Unveils World's First Brain-to-Robot Platform
items.summary: Chinese startup BrainCo launched the world's first brain-to-robot platform, allowing users to control robots via an EEG headset that decodes brain signals into actions.

items.title: National Protests Erupt Against U.S. Data Center Buildout
items.summary: Opponents of rapid data center expansion held coordinated protests at 125 locations across the U.S. on Saturday, marking the first national effort against AI infrastructure growth.

items.title: Ecopetrol Confirms Cyberattack Stealing 3,300 User Accounts
items.summary: Colombian energy company Ecopetrol reported a cyberattack that stole data tied to approximately 3,300 user accounts, warning of potential material adverse financial impact.

items.title: U.S. Justice Department Allows Federal Employees to Download TikTok
items.summary: The U.S. Justice Department announced Friday that federal employees can now download the short-video app TikTok on government devices.

items.title: Patreon Shifts to Blocking AI Bots Instead of Just Requesting Compliance
items.summary: Patreon stopped asking AI bots not to scrape content and began actively blocking them using Cloudflare to prevent unauthorized AI training on creator content.

items.title: NHTSA Calls for Autonomous Vehicle Emergency Response Improvements
items.summary: The NHTSA recently issued a call for autonomous vehicle companies to improve how their cars respond in emergency situations.

items.title: Apple Updates Maps Ad Policies to Block Crypto ATMs and Bail Bond Ads
items.summary: Starting mid-July, Apple updated its policies to prohibit ads from home service providers, cryptocurrency ATMs, and bail bond services on Apple Maps in the U.S. and Canada.

items.title: AWS Customers Hit with Shockingly High Bills Up to $1.5 Trillion
items.summary: Amazon Web Services customers recently received shockingly high monthly bills, with some reports indicating charges as high as $1.5 trillion due to configuration errors.

items.title: San Francisco Orders Apple and Google to Remove AI Undressing Apps
items.summary: San Francisco has ordered tech giants Apple and Google to remove 'nudify' apps from their platforms to protect users from AI-generated explicit material.
